# HTML Plan Host

> Publish a self-contained HTML document and get back a public, shareable URL.
> Built for coding agents: no account or pre-shared key needed — mint your own
> throwaway workspace in one call. Pure HTTP, zero dependencies.

## Publish in two steps (any HTTP client)

API host: https://vibrant-barracuda-527.convex.site

1. POST /provision  ->  { "apiKey": "phk_...", "claimUrl": "..." }
   Mints an anonymous workspace. No auth required. Rate-limited per IP.

2. POST /plans
   Header: Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>
   Header: content-type: application/json
   Body:   { "html": "<!doctype html>...", "title"?: "...", "slug"?: "...", "expiresInDays"?: N }
   ->  { "url": ".../p/<slug>", "title": "...", "updated": false,
         "temporary": true, "claimUrl": ".../claim/<token>" }   // last two only for anonymous workspaces

The returned `url` is the ONE link to share: it renders your HTML, and a human
can flip on inline commenting with a button — no account needed. Do NOT build or
share any other view/comment URL for the plan; commenting lives on `url` itself.
Give the user exactly: the `url` (plus the `claimUrl` below if present). That's it.

IMPORTANT — when you self-provisioned (anonymous workspace), the publish response
includes "temporary": true and a "claimUrl". ALWAYS give the user BOTH the `url`
AND the `claimUrl`, and tell them: this plan is temporary and will expire in ~7
days unless they open the claimUrl, sign in, and claim it — which moves it into
their permanent account. The claimUrl rides with every publish, so you don't need
to save it from /provision; just pass along whatever the latest publish returned.

## Shell example

```sh
HOST=https://vibrant-barracuda-527.convex.site
KEY=$(curl -s -X POST "$HOST/provision" | sed -n 's/.*"apiKey":"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p')
curl -s -X POST "$HOST/plans" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"My Plan","html":"<!doctype html><h1>Hello</h1>"}'
```

## Notes

- Provide a full, self-contained HTML document. Inline all CSS in a `<style>`
  tag — there is no external asset hosting.
- `slug` gives a stable, updatable URL: re-publishing the same slug overwrites
  the plan in place. Omit it for a random unguessable slug.
- Humans comment on any section (no account) right on the page `url` returns —
  there's a "Comments" button that turns on click-to-comment.
- Need the raw HTML bytes (machine-to-machine read)? Same slug on the API host:
  https://vibrant-barracuda-527.convex.site/p/<slug>
- Anonymous workspaces are throwaway (plans expire in ~7 days) until a human
  opens the `claimUrl` to claim them into a permanent account. Don't drop the
  claimUrl on the floor — the user needs it to keep the plan.
- Alternatively, the user can sign up at the app first and give you their own API
  key; then plans are permanent immediately and there's nothing to claim.

## Links

- App / sign-in: https://html-plan-host.vercel.app
- Source (self-hostable): https://github.com/Dan-Cleary/html-plan-host
